items.title: The $7 Trillion AI Boom Is Running Out of Power
items.description: Forget the chips. Forget the code. The most expensive, in-demand commodity in the entire $3-trillion AI revolution is not a patented algorithm or a new Nvidia GPU. It's power. items.title: Colombia's Cocaine Trade Now Outearns Its Oil Exports
items.description: Drug lords in Colombia are making more money from cocaine than the government is making from crude oil sales, a report from a Colombian university has found. At $16.5 billion for 2024, cocaine revenues surpassed oil export revenues, which stood at around $15 billion that year, UPI reported, citing t

items.title: Geothermal Could Power 65 Million U.S. Homes by 2050, DOE Says
items.description: “It’s going to be the decade of geothermal,” Cindy Taff, chief executive of geothermal company Sage Geosystems, told The Hill in February of 2025. Over a year later, it is becoming increasingly evident that Taff is definitely onto something. Although geothermal energy is still a tiny sector and face

items.title: Asia's Crude Buying Spree Is Running Out of Steam
items.description: Asian refiners have reduced their spot purchases of Middle East crude for loading this month and next, following three weeks in which they had purchased millions of barrels of UAE, Saudi, and Iraqi crude. items.title: SpaceX Wants to Fuel Its Mars Ambitions With Its Own Gas Pipeline
items.description: SpaceX has spent years trying to reinvent rockets. Now it's coming after midstream. The aerospace giant plans to begin construction next month on an eight-mile natural gas pipeline dubbed "Starpipe" that will feed its Starbase launch facility in South Texas, according to filings reviewed by Reuters.

items.description: Lithium carbonate futures in China fell ~10% over two trading sessions, dropping to a 10-week low of approximately 157,000 yuan ($23,175) per tonne on Tuesday following market speculation that China’s EV battery giant, Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. items.title: Gas Prices Are Falling. Your July 4 Fill-Up Still Won't Feel Cheap.
items.description: The good news is that gasoline prices have been falling for six straight weeks. The bad news? Americans are still heading into one of the most expensive Independence Day weekends they've ever seen. items.title: Russia Turns Asia’s Oil Shock Into an Indonesian Opening
items.description: Russia has emerged as one of the clearest commercial beneficiaries of the US-Israel war with Iran. items.title: Major Saudi Oil Terminal Roars Back to Life as Gulf Supply Recovery Accelerates
items.description: Saudi Arabia's biggest oil export terminal is finally stirring back to life, and that's a much bigger deal than another headline about the Strait of Hormuz reopening. items.title: AI Boom Forces Tennessee to Plan Massive Natural Gas Expansion

items.title: UK Energy Secretary Vetoed Plan to Boost Oil Output for Defense Funds
items.description: UK Energy Secretary Ed Miliband has vetoed a plan by the Treasury to boost North Sea oil and gas drilling in order to fund part of Britain’s increased defense funding needs, The Telegraph reported on Thursday, citing a government source. items.title: AI Data Center Boom Could Drive Fuel Cell Market to $30 Billion by 2030
items.description: Data center developers are scrambling for reliable power, turning away from congested grids and toward on-site fuel cells. items.title: Middle East Fuel Oil Exports Set to Hit Four-Month High
items.description: Fuel oil exports from the Middle East are expected to rebound to a four-month high this month as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz picks up and key fuel oil producers Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and Oman have moved to increase shipments from ports outside the Persian Gulf.
